     9→# Metrics - Epic Breakdown
    10→
    11→## Overview
    12→
    13→This document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for Metrics, decomposing the requirements from the PRD, UX Design if it exists, and Architecture requirements into implementable stories.
    14→
    15→## Requirements Inventory
    16→
    17→### Functional Requirements
    18→
    19→- FR1: User can view the current BTC/USD price, updated in real-time
    20→- FR2: User can see price updates reflected in the browser tab title
    21→- FR3: User can see a visual indication of price direction (up/down) on each update
    22→- FR4: User can view the 24-hour price change as both a percentage and absolute value
    23→- FR5: User can distinguish positive and negative price changes via color coding
    24→- FR6: User can view the current BTC market capitalization
    25→- FR7: System refreshes supplementary market data (24h change, market cap) periodically without user action
    26→- FR8: User can view a sparkline chart showing BTC price over the last 24 hours
    27→- FR9: User can hover over the chart to see the price and time at a specific data point
    28→- FR10: Chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period
    29→- FR11: System connects to the primary WebSocket data source (Binance) on page load
    30→- FR12: System falls back to a secondary WebSocket source (CoinCap) if the primary fails
    31→- FR13: System automatically reconnects when a WebSocket connection drops
    32→- FR14: User can see a visual indicator when the data connection is lost or reconnecting
    33→- FR15: User can see a stale data indicator when displayed price may be outdated
    34→- FR16: System falls back to REST API polling when WebSocket is unavailable
    35→- FR17: User sees a dark-themed interface with crypto-aesthetic styling on page load
    36→- FR18: User experiences no flash of unstyled or light-themed content on initial load
    37→- FR19: User sees smooth entry animations when data first loads
    38→- FR20: User sees no layout shift as dynamic content populates
    39→- FR21: User can view all dashboard data on desktop screens with a horizontal layout
    40→- FR22: User can view all dashboard data on mobile screens with a vertically stacked layout
    41→- FR23: Dashboard is usable and readable across all supported modern browsers
    42→
    43→### NonFunctional Requirements
    44→
    45→- NFR1: Page load (DOMContentLoaded) completes in < 2 seconds on a 4G connection
    46→- NFR2: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) occurs in < 1.5 seconds
    47→- NFR3: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) = 0 — no visible layout shift during data hydration
    48→- NFR4: Price updates render to DOM within 1 second of WebSocket message receipt
    49→- NFR5: All animations run at 60 FPS with no dropped frames
    50→- NFR6: Lighthouse performance score ≥ 90
    51→- NFR7: Total hydrated JavaScript bundle < 100KB gzipped
    52→- NFR8: Chart library (lightweight-charts) < 50KB gzipped
    53→- NFR9: Binance WebSocket connection establishes within 500ms of island hydration under normal network conditions
    54→- NFR10: WebSocket reconnection uses exponential backoff starting at 1 second, capping at 30 seconds
    55→- NFR11: CoinGecko REST API polling interval ≥ 60 seconds to stay within free tier rate limits (30 req/min)
    56→- NFR12: System tolerates CoinGecko API unavailability by displaying last known data with a timestamp
    57→- NFR13: Binance 24-hour connection expiry handled transparently as a standard reconnect cycle
    58→- NFR14: Fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention
    59→- NFR15: Dashboard remains functional (displays last known data) when all external APIs are unreachable
    60→- NFR16: No JavaScript errors or white screens on WebSocket disconnection
    61→- NFR17: Browser tab remains stable during extended sessions (no memory leaks over 24+ hours)
    62→
    63→### Additional Requirements
    64→
    65→- Starter template: `npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict` — specified in Architecture as Epic 1 Story 1
    66→- Tailwind CSS v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin with CSS-first `@theme` configuration (no `tailwind.config.js`)
    67→- Nano Stores (~1KB) as the reactive state layer shared across Astro islands (Astro's officially recommended approach)
    68→- Vanilla TypeScript islands — no UI framework; islands use Astro `<script>` tags with `client:load` for direct DOM updates
    69→- WebSocket ConnectionManager as a singleton managing the entire WebSocket lifecycle with fallback chain
    70→- `theme-constants.ts` for lightweight-charts JS API color sync with Tailwind `@theme` tokens (duplicated values, acceptable for v1)
    71→- Shared TypeScript interfaces in `src/lib/types.ts`: `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, `ConnectionStatus`
    72→- Unidirectional data flow: External API → Service → Store → Component → DOM
    73→- Memory leak prevention: listener cleanup on reconnect, chart data capped at 24 entries, timer reference management with `AbortController`
    74→- ESLint (`eslint-plugin-astro`) + Prettier (`prettier-plugin-astro`) + Vitest from first commit
    75→- Playwright for e2e smoke test ("page loads, price appears")
    76→- Package manager: npm (explicit choice)
    77→- Node 22+ required (Astro 6.x)
    78→- Co-located test files with `.test.ts` suffix
    79→- No barrel files (`utils/index.ts`) — import directly from specific modules
    80→- Recursive `setTimeout` for REST polling (not `setInterval`) to prevent drift on slow responses
    81→
    82→### UX Design Requirements
    83→
    84→- UX-DR1: Implement design token system — semantic color tokens (`bg-base` #0a0a0f, `bg-card` #12121a, `bg-card-hover` #1a1a2e, `accent-primary` #00f5ff cyan, `accent-secondary` #8b5cf6 purple, `accent-glow` rgba(0,245,255,0.15), `price-up` #22c55e, `price-down` #ef4444, `price-flash-up` #00f5ff, `price-flash-down` #ef4444, `text-primary` #f0f0f0, `text-secondary` #a0a0b0, `text-muted` #606070, `status-live` #22c55e, `status-reconnecting` #f59e0b, `status-stale` #ef4444) via Tailwind `@theme` directives in `src/styles/global.css`
    85→- UX-DR2: Implement typography system — JetBrains Mono (`font-price`) for all numerical data, Inter (`font-label`) for labels/status text. Type scale: price 48px/36px weight 600, values 20-24px weight 500, card labels 12px Inter weight 400 `text-secondary`, status 11px Inter weight 400 `text-muted`, chart tooltip 13px JetBrains Mono. Line heights: 1.1 prices, 1.4 labels, 1.3 status
    86→- UX-DR3: Implement glassmorphism card pattern (`.card-glass`) — `bg-card` background + `backdrop-blur(12px)` + 1px border `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` + 16px border-radius + optional `accent-secondary` glow (`box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(139,92,246,0.1)`)
    87→- UX-DR4: Implement Price Card component — label "Bitcoin BTC/USD", live price at 48px desktop/36px mobile, change row with arrow symbol (↑/↓) + percentage (+2.3%) + absolute (+$1,534.22) in `price-up`/`price-down` colors. States: Live (color flash 400ms), Stale (badge), Loading (label visible, data fades in 300ms)
    88→- UX-DR5: Implement Market Cap Card component — label "Market Cap", value abbreviated ($1.34T) at 24px weight 500, secondary "24h Range" label with range value at 14px. Silent 60s refresh, no flash animation
    89→- UX-DR6: Implement Chart Card component — lightweight-charts with line color `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), area gradient (rgba(0,245,255,0.1) top, transparent bottom), grid lines rgba(255,255,255,0.03), crosshair rgba(255,255,255,0.3), transparent background. Hover crosshair with price+time tooltip (13px JetBrains Mono). Draw animation 500ms. Full width spanning both grid columns on desktop
    90→- UX-DR7: Implement Status Indicator component — 6px colored dot + state text at 11px `text-muted`, centered bottom of viewport. States: Live (green dot + "Live"), Reconnecting (amber pulsing dot 1.5s cycle + "Reconnecting..."), Stale (red static dot + "Connection lost"), Fallback (green dot + "Live — CoinCap"). State transitions fade 300ms
    91→- UX-DR8: Implement Page Shell — full-viewport `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) background, CSS Grid `1.5fr 1fr` desktop / single column mobile, 640px breakpoint, max-width 720px container, vertical centering on desktop, top-aligned on mobile. Spacing: 32px page padding desktop, 16px mobile, 24px card gaps
    92→- UX-DR9: Implement animation system — data fade-in 300ms ease-out, chart draw 500ms ease-out, price tick flash 400ms ease-out, state transitions 300ms ease-in-out, reconnecting pulse 1.5s ease-in-out, card hover glow 300ms ease. No animation exceeds 500ms. No bounce/spring/overshoot. All respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (0ms durations). Price flash throttled to max 1 per 200ms
    93→- UX-DR10: Implement FOUC prevention — set `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) on `<html>` element directly, dark theme as first paint. Empty glassmorphism cards with labels as the loading state (no spinners, no skeletons). Reserved space for all dynamic content (CLS = 0)
    94→- UX-DR11: Implement responsive design — desktop-first with single 640px breakpoint (`sm:` Tailwind prefix). Price 48px→36px, grid collapses to single column. No `vh` units on mobile (use `dvh` or `min-h-screen`). Chart auto-resizes via `ResizeObserver`. No features hidden on mobile. CSS-only responsive logic
    95→- UX-DR12: Implement accessibility — `<section aria-label="Bitcoin price">` with `<output aria-live="polite">` for price, `<section aria-label="Market data">` for market cap, `<section aria-label="24 hour price chart">` with `aria-hidden="true"` on chart canvas, `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` for Status Indicator, `<html lang="en">`. Direction arrows (↑/↓) alongside color coding. All text meets AA contrast minimum
    96→
    97→### FR Coverage Map
    98→
    99→- FR1: Epic 2 — Live BTC/USD price display
   100→- FR2: Epic 2 — Price in browser tab title
   101→- FR3: Epic 2 — Price direction indicator (up/down)
   102→- FR4: Epic 2 — 24h change (percentage + absolute)
   103→- FR5: Epic 2 — Color-coded positive/negative changes
   104→- FR6: Epic 3 — Market cap display
   105→- FR7: Epic 3 — Periodic market data refresh
   106→- FR8: Epic 3 — 24h sparkline chart
   107→- FR9: Epic 3 — Chart hover tooltip (price + time)
   108→- FR10: Epic 3 — Hourly chart data points
   109→- FR11: Epic 2 — Binance WebSocket connection on load
   110→- FR12: Epic 4 — CoinCap fallback WebSocket
   111→- FR13: Epic 4 — Auto-reconnection on drop
   112→- FR14: Epic 4 — Reconnecting visual indicator
   113→- FR15: Epic 4 — Stale data indicator
   114→- FR16: Epic 4 — REST polling fallback
   115→- FR17: Epic 1 — Dark-themed crypto aesthetic
   116→- FR18: Epic 1 — No FOUC
   117→- FR19: Epic 2 — Entry animations
   118→- FR20: Epic 1 — No layout shift
   119→- FR21: Epic 1 — Desktop horizontal layout
   120→- FR22: Epic 1 — Mobile stacked layout
   121→- FR23: Epic 1 — Cross-browser support
   122→
   123→## Epic List
   124→
   125→### Epic 1: Project Foundation & Dashboard Shell
   126→The user sees a polished, responsive dark crypto dashboard that loads instantly — the visual identity and layout are complete, ready for live data.
   127→**FRs covered:** FR17, FR18, FR20, FR21, FR22, FR23
   128→
   129→### Epic 2: Live Bitcoin Price Display
   130→The user can glance at the dashboard (or tab title) and instantly know the current BTC price, its direction, and 24h change — updated in real-time.
   131→**FRs covered:** FR1, FR2, FR3, FR4, FR5, FR11, FR19
   132→
   133→### Epic 3: Market Context & Price Chart
   134→The user gets the full market picture — market cap, 24h range, and a 24h sparkline chart with hover tooltips — completing the dashboard's information offering.
   135→**FRs covered:** FR6, FR7, FR8, FR9, FR10
   136→
   137→### Epic 4: Connection Resilience & Reliability
   138→The dashboard never breaks — WebSocket failures are handled silently with fallback sources, automatic reconnection, and clear stale data indication. The user can trust the data is always there.
   139→**FRs covered:** FR12, FR13, FR14, FR15, FR16
   140→
   141→## Epic 1: Project Foundation & Dashboard Shell
   142→
   143→The user sees a polished, responsive dark crypto dashboard that loads instantly — the visual identity and layout are complete, ready for live data.
   144→
   145→### Story 1.1: Project Initialization & Tooling Setup
   146→
   147→As a developer,
   148→I want a fully scaffolded Astro project with TypeScript, Tailwind, and code quality tooling,
   149→So that I can start building components with a consistent, well-configured dev environment from the first commit.
   150→
   151→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   152→
   153→**Given** a clean project directory
   154→**When** the initialization commands are run
   155→**Then** Astro 6.x is installed with the minimal template and TypeScript strict mode
   156→**And** Tailwind CSS v4 is configured via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin in `astro.config.mjs`
   157→**And** `lightweight-charts`, `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono`, `@fontsource/inter`, and `nanostores` are installed
   158→**And** ESLint with `eslint-plugin-astro`, Prettier with `prettier-plugin-astro`, and Vitest are configured
   159→**And** the project structure follows Architecture spec: `src/pages/`, `src/layouts/`, `src/components/`, `src/lib/stores/`, `src/lib/services/`, `src/lib/utils/`, `src/styles/`
   160→**And** `src/lib/types.ts` defines `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, and `ConnectionStatus` interfaces
   161→**And** `npm run dev` starts the Astro dev server successfully
   162→**And** `npm run build` produces a static output in `dist/`
   163→
   164→### Story 1.2: Design Token System & Typography
   165→
   166→As a developer,
   167→I want a complete design token system with semantic colors, typography, and shared theme constants,
   168→So that all components use a consistent dark crypto visual identity and the chart library stays in sync.
   169→
   170→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   171→
   172→**Given** the Astro project from Story 1.1
   173→**When** `src/styles/global.css` is created with Tailwind `@theme` directives
   174→**Then** all semantic color tokens are defined: `bg-base` (#0a0a0f), `bg-card` (#12121a), `bg-card-hover` (#1a1a2e), `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), `accent-secondary` (#8b5cf6), `accent-glow` (rgba(0,245,255,0.15)), `price-up` (#22c55e), `price-down` (#ef4444), `price-flash-up` (#00f5ff), `price-flash-down` (#ef4444), `text-primary` (#f0f0f0), `text-secondary` (#a0a0b0), `text-muted` (#606070), `status-live` (#22c55e), `status-reconnecting` (#f59e0b), `status-stale` (#ef4444)
   175→**And** JetBrains Mono is configured as `font-price` and Inter as `font-label` via `@fontsource` imports
   176→**And** the type scale is defined: price 48px weight 600, values 20-24px weight 500, labels 12px weight 400, status 11px weight 400
   177→**And** `src/lib/theme-constants.ts` exports duplicated color values for lightweight-charts JS API configuration
   178→**And** `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, and `.status-badge` `@apply` compositions are defined (max 3)
   179→**And** CSS custom properties for runtime animation values (`--price-flash-up`, `--price-flash-down`) are defined in `:root`
   180→**And** tokens are usable via Tailwind utility classes in any component
   181→
   182→### Story 1.3: Page Shell with Responsive Layout
   183→
   184→As a user,
   185→I want the dashboard to load instantly with a dark background and a responsive card grid layout,
   186→So that I never see a flash of white and the page looks polished on both desktop and mobile.
   187→
   188→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   189→
   190→**Given** a user navigates to the dashboard URL
   191→**When** the page loads
   192→**Then** `<html>` has `lang="en"` and `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) set directly to prevent FOUC (FR18)
   193→**And** `Layout.astro` renders a full-viewport dark background with a centered content container (max-width 720px)
   194→**And** the layout uses CSS Grid with `grid-template-columns: 1.5fr 1fr` and 24px gaps on desktop (≥640px) (FR21)
   195→**And** the layout collapses to a single-column stack with 16px horizontal padding on mobile (<640px) (FR22)
   196→**And** the container is vertically centered on desktop and top-aligned on mobile
   197→**And** no `vh` units are used on mobile — `dvh` or `min-h-screen` is used instead
   198→**And** responsive behavior is CSS-only with no JavaScript (Tailwind `sm:` breakpoint)
   199→**And** the page renders correctly on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge latest 2 versions (FR23)
   200→**And** `index.astro` imports `Layout.astro` and serves as the single page entry
   201→
   202→### Story 1.4: Empty Dashboard Cards
   203→
   204→As a user,
   205→I want to see the dashboard layout with labeled card placeholders immediately on page load,
   206→So that the page feels complete and no layout shift occurs when data arrives later.
   207→
   208→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   209→
   210→**Given** a user loads the dashboard
   211→**When** the static shell renders (before any data)
   212→**Then** four glassmorphism cards are visible: Price Card, Market Cap Card, Chart Card, Status Indicator area
   213→**And** each card uses the `.card-glass` pattern: `bg-card` + `backdrop-blur(12px)` + 1px border rgba(255,255,255,0.08) + 16px border-radius
   214→**And** Price Card displays label "Bitcoin BTC/USD" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
   215→**And** Market Cap Card displays label "Market Cap" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
   216→**And** Chart Card displays label "24h Chart" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px and spans full grid width on desktop
   217→**And** each card has reserved space (fixed height) for its data values so CLS = 0 when data populates (FR20)
   218→**And** Price Card has `<section aria-label="Bitcoin price">` with `<output aria-live="polite">` placeholder
   219→**And** Market Cap Card has `<section aria-label="Market data">`
   220→**And** Chart Card has `<section aria-label="24 hour price chart">`
   221→**And** Status Indicator area has `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` at the bottom of the viewport
   222→**And** the dark-themed crypto aesthetic is visually complete — near-black background, glassmorphism cards, neon accents visible (FR17)
   223→
   224→## Epic 2: Live Bitcoin Price Display
   225→
   226→The user can glance at the dashboard (or tab title) and instantly know the current BTC price, its direction, and 24h change — updated in real-time.
   227→
   228→### Story 2.1: Nano Stores & Number Formatting Utilities
   229→
   230→As a developer,
   231→I want reactive stores and consistent number formatting helpers in place,
   232→So that all components share a single source of truth for data and display values identically.
   233→
   234→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   235→
   236→**Given** the project structure from Epic 1
   237→**When** the store and utility files are created
   238→**Then** `src/lib/stores/price-store.ts` exports `priceStore` as `atom<PriceTick | null>(null)`
   239→**And** `src/lib/stores/status-store.ts` exports `statusStore` as `atom<ConnectionStatus>('connecting')`
   240→**And** `src/lib/stores/market-store.ts` exports `marketStore` as `atom<MarketData | null>(null)`
   241→**And** `src/lib/stores/chart-store.ts` exports `chartStore` as `atom<ChartPoint[]>([])`
   242→**And** every store is typed with explicit generics and initializes to `null` or empty (never undefined)
   243→**And** `src/lib/utils/format-utils.ts` exports: `formatPrice()` → `$68,432.17`, `formatPercent()` → `+2.3%` or `-1.8%`, `formatAbsoluteChange()` → `+$1,534.22`, `formatMarketCap()` → `$1.34T`, `formatRange()` → `$66.9k — $68.9k`, `formatChartTooltip()` → `$68,432.17 at 14:30`
   244→**And** all formatters use `Intl.NumberFormat` as the base — no manual string building
   245→**And** `format-utils.test.ts` covers all formatters with positive, negative, zero, and edge cases
   246→**And** tests pass via `npm run test`
   247→
   248→### Story 2.2: WebSocket Connection Manager (Binance)
   249→
   250→As a user,
   251→I want the dashboard to connect to a live Bitcoin data source automatically on page load,
   252→So that I see real-time price updates without any action.
   253→
   254→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   255→
   256→**Given** the dashboard page loads and islands hydrate
   257→**When** the ConnectionManager initializes
   258→**Then** it connects to Binance WebSocket at `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` (FR11)
   259→**And** raw Binance messages (`{ p: "68432.17", ... }`) are normalized to `PriceTick` format: `{ price: number, timestamp: number, direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral' }`
   260→**And** direction is computed by comparing to the previous price
   261→**And** normalized `PriceTick` is written to `priceStore`
   262→**And** `statusStore` is updated to `'live'` on successful connection
   263→**And** `statusStore` is set to `'connecting'` during initial connection attempt
   264→**And** the ConnectionManager is a singleton — only one WebSocket connection exists at any time
   265→**And** `connection-manager.ts` never exposes raw WebSocket data to consumers
   266→**And** old WebSocket listeners are cleaned up before creating new connections (memory leak prevention)
   267→**And** services initialize via a `<script>` block in `Layout.astro`
   268→**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers message normalization and direction computation
   269→
   270→### Story 2.3: Live Price Card with Direction Indicator
   271→
   272→As a user,
   273→I want to see the current BTC price updating live with visual direction feedback,
   274→So that I can glance at the dashboard and instantly know the price and whether it's moving up or down.
   275→
   276→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   277→
   278→**Given** the Price Card island is hydrated and priceStore has data
   279→**When** a new PriceTick arrives in priceStore
   280→**Then** the price displays in `font-price` at 48px desktop / 36px mobile, weight 600, `text-primary` color (FR1)
   281→**And** a direction arrow is shown: `↑` in `price-up` green for up, `↓` in `price-down` red for down (FR3, FR5)
   282→**And** the price text flashes cyan (`price-flash-up`) on price increase or red (`price-flash-down`) on decrease, fading to white over 400ms ease-out (UX-DR9)
   283→**And** flash frequency is throttled to max 1 per 200ms to prevent rapid flashing
   284→**And** 24h change displays as percentage (`+2.3%`) and absolute value (`+$1,534.22`) using format-utils (FR4)
   285→**And** 24h change values use `price-up` green for positive and `price-down` red for negative (FR5)
   286→**And** all number formatting goes through `format-utils.ts` — no inline formatting
   287→**And** on initial data load, the price fades in with a 300ms ease-out animation (FR19)
   288→**And** all animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant transitions when enabled)
   289→**And** the `<output aria-live="polite">` element updates so screen readers announce price changes
   290→
   291→### Story 2.4: Tab Title Price Updates
   292→
   293→As a user,
   294→I want the browser tab title to show the current BTC price,
   295→So that I can see the price without switching to the Metrics tab.
   296→
   297→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   298→
   299→**Given** the dashboard is live and priceStore is receiving updates
   300→**When** a new PriceTick arrives
   301→**Then** the browser tab title updates to show the formatted price, e.g. `$68,432.17 — Metrics` (FR2)
   302→**And** the tab title uses the same `formatPrice()` function from format-utils for consistency
   303→**And** the tab title updates on every price tick (not throttled separately from the display)
   304→**And** if priceStore is `null` (no data yet), the tab title shows `Metrics` without a price
   305→
   306→## Epic 3: Market Context & Price Chart
   307→
   308→The user gets the full market picture — market cap, 24h range, and a 24h sparkline chart with hover tooltips — completing the dashboard's information offering.
   309→
   310→### Story 3.1: Market Data Service (CoinGecko REST Polling)
   311→
   312→As a user,
   313→I want the dashboard to fetch market data automatically in the background,
   314→So that I see up-to-date market cap, 24h change, and chart data without any action.
   315→
   316→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   317→
   318→**Given** the dashboard page loads and services initialize
   319→**When** the MarketDataService starts
   320→**Then** it fetches market cap, 24h change (percentage + absolute), 24h high/low, and hourly historical price data from CoinGecko REST API
   321→**And** `marketStore` is populated with a `MarketData` object: `{ marketCap, change24h, changePercent24h, high24h, low24h }`
   322→**And** `chartStore` is populated with an array of `ChartPoint` objects: `{ time: number, value: number }` for hourly data
   323→**And** `chartStore` is capped at 24 entries maximum — oldest point shifted when appending new data
   324→**And** polling uses recursive `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) with a 60-second interval (NFR11)
   325→**And** on CoinGecko API failure, last known store values are retained — stores are never cleared (NFR12)
   326→**And** API errors are logged via `console.warn()` with endpoint and status code — no user-facing error UI
   327→**And** timer references are stored and cleared before scheduling new timers (memory leak prevention)
   328→**And** `market-data-service.test.ts` covers data mapping, store updates, and error retention behavior
   329→
   330→### Story 3.2: Market Cap Card
   331→
   332→As a user,
   333→I want to see the current Bitcoin market cap and 24h price range at a glance,
   334→So that I have broader market context alongside the live price.
   335→
   336→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   337→
   338→**Given** the Market Cap Card island is hydrated and marketStore has data
   339→**When** marketStore updates with new MarketData
   340→**Then** market cap displays as an abbreviated value (e.g. `$1.34T`) in `font-price`, `text-primary`, 24px weight 500 (FR6)
   341→**And** "Market Cap" label is shown in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
   342→**And** 24h range displays as `$66.9k — $68.9k` in `font-price`, `text-primary`, 14px
   343→**And** "24h Range" secondary label is shown in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
   344→**And** all values use `formatMarketCap()` and `formatRange()` from format-utils — no inline formatting
   345→**And** values update silently every 60s with no flash animation (FR7)
   346→**And** on initial data load, values fade in with 300ms ease-out animation
   347→**And** when marketStore is `null` (no data yet), labels are visible but value areas are empty (reserved space)
   348→
   349→### Story 3.3: 24h Sparkline Chart with Hover Tooltip
   350→
   351→As a user,
   352→I want to see a 24-hour price trend chart that I can hover for details,
   353→So that I can quickly read the trend shape and check specific price points.
   354→
   355→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   356→
   357→**Given** the Chart Card island is hydrated and chartStore has hourly data
   358→**When** chartStore populates with ChartPoint array
   359→**Then** a lightweight-charts area series renders with line color `accent-primary` (#00f5ff) (FR8)
   360→**And** area fill uses gradient: rgba(0,245,255,0.1) top to transparent bottom
   361→**And** chart grid lines are rgba(255,255,255,0.03), crosshair rgba(255,255,255,0.3)
   362→**And** chart background is transparent (card provides the background)
   363→**And** all chart colors are sourced from `theme-constants.ts` — no hardcoded values in component
   364→**And** chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period (FR10)
   365→**And** on hover, a crosshair appears with a tooltip showing price and time formatted as `$68,432.17 at 14:30` using `formatChartTooltip()` (FR9)
   366→**And** on initial data load, the chart draws left-to-right with a 500ms ease-out animation (UX-DR6)
   367→**And** the chart auto-resizes via lightweight-charts built-in resize handling on viewport change
   368→**And** the chart card spans full grid width (`grid-column: 1 / -1`) on desktop
   369→**And** chart canvas has `aria-hidden="true"` — price data is accessible via Price Card
   370→**And** animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant draw when enabled)
   371→**And** when chartStore updates with new data, the chart appends points without full redraw or flicker
   372→
   373→## Epic 4: Connection Resilience & Reliability
   374→
   375→The dashboard never breaks — WebSocket failures are handled silently with fallback sources, automatic reconnection, and clear stale data indication. The user can trust the data is always there.
   376→
   377→### Story 4.1: WebSocket Reconnection with Exponential Backoff
   378→
   379→As a user,
   380→I want the dashboard to automatically recover from connection drops without my intervention,
   381→So that the live price feed resumes seamlessly after network interruptions.
   382→
   383→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   384→
   385→**Given** the ConnectionManager has an active Binance WebSocket connection
   386→**When** the WebSocket connection drops (error, close, or network failure)
   387→**Then** `statusStore` transitions to `'reconnecting'` (FR14)
   388→**And** reconnection attempts begin with exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s cap (NFR10)
   389→**And** on successful reconnection, `statusStore` transitions back to `'live'`
   390→**And** the previous WebSocket instance is fully closed (`ws.close()`) and its reference nulled before creating a new connection (memory leak prevention, NFR17)
   391→**And** no JavaScript errors or white screens occur during disconnection or reconnection (NFR16)
   392→**And** Binance 24-hour connection expiry is handled as a standard disconnect/reconnect cycle — no special logic (NFR13)
   393→**And** the last known price remains displayed in the Price Card during reconnection (NFR15)
   394→**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers backoff timing, listener cleanup, and reconnection state transitions
   395→
   396→### Story 4.2: CoinCap Fallback & REST Polling Fallback
   397→
   398→As a user,
   399→I want the dashboard to try alternative data sources if the primary one fails,
   400→So that I always see price data regardless of which service is available.
   401→
   402→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   403→
   404→**Given** the ConnectionManager fails to connect or reconnect to Binance
   405→**When** Binance WebSocket is unavailable after reconnection attempts
   406→**Then** the ConnectionManager attempts to connect to CoinCap WebSocket at `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin` (FR12)
   407→**And** CoinCap messages (`{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }`) are normalized to the same `PriceTick` format
   408→**And** on successful CoinCap connection, `statusStore` transitions to `'fallback'`
   409→**And** fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention (NFR14)
   410→**And** if both Binance and CoinCap WebSockets fail, the system falls back to REST polling via the MarketDataService for price data (FR16)
   411→**And** during REST-only mode, `statusStore` reflects `'stale'` if no update received within 30 seconds
   412→**And** the dashboard remains functional displaying last known data when all external APIs are unreachable (NFR15)
   413→**And** when a higher-priority source becomes available again, the ConnectionManager reconnects to it
   414→**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers the full fallback chain: Binance → CoinCap → REST
   415→
   416→### Story 4.3: Status Indicator & Stale Data Badge
   417→
   418→As a user,
   419→I want to see a subtle connection status indicator and know when displayed data may be outdated,
   420→So that I can trust the data when it's live and be aware when it's stale.
   421→
   422→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   423→
   424→**Given** the Status Indicator island is hydrated and subscribes to statusStore
   425→**When** statusStore changes value
   426→**Then** the indicator displays at the bottom center of the viewport as a 6px dot + text at 11px `text-muted` (UX-DR7)
   427→**And** Live state shows: green dot (`status-live`) + "Live" text — barely visible, quiet good state
   428→**And** Reconnecting state shows: amber dot (`status-reconnecting`) + "Reconnecting..." text with dot pulsing animation (1.5s ease-in-out cycle, opacity 0.4–1.0) (FR14)
   429→**And** Stale state shows: red dot (`status-stale`) + "Connection lost" text, static (no animation)
   430→**And** Fallback state shows: green dot (`status-live`) + "Live — CoinCap" text
   431→**And** state transitions fade over 300ms ease-in-out — no abrupt switches
   432→**And** when statusStore is `'stale'` (no price update for >30 seconds), a "Stale" badge appears on the Price Card in `status-stale` color (FR15)
   433→**And** the stale badge does not hide or replace the price value — it's an addition
   434→**And** when connection restores and a new price tick arrives, the stale badge fades out over 300ms
   435→**And** all animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant transitions, no pulsing)
   436→**And** the Status Indicator uses `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` so screen readers announce state changes
   437→

---
stepsCompleted: [step-01-validate-prerequisites, step-02-design-epics, step-03-create-stories, step-04-final-validation]

# Metrics - Epic Breakdown

## Overview

This document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for Metrics, decomposing the requirements from the PRD, UX Design if it exists, and Architecture requirements into implementable stories.

## Requirements Inventory

### Functional Requirements

- FR1: User can view the current BTC/USD price, updated in real-time
- FR2: User can see price updates reflected in the browser tab title
- FR3: User can see a visual indication of price direction (up/down) on each update
- FR4: User can view the 24-hour price change as both a percentage and absolute value
- FR5: User can distinguish positive and negative price changes via color coding
- FR6: User can view the current BTC market capitalization
- FR7: System refreshes supplementary market data (24h change, market cap) periodically without user action
- FR8: User can view a sparkline chart showing BTC price over the last 24 hours
- FR9: User can hover over the chart to see the price and time at a specific data point
- FR10: Chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period
- FR11: System connects to the primary WebSocket data source (Binance) on page load
- FR12: System falls back to a secondary WebSocket source (CoinCap) if the primary fails
- FR13: System automatically reconnects when a WebSocket connection drops
- FR14: User can see a visual indicator when the data connection is lost or reconnecting
- FR15: User can see a stale data indicator when displayed price may be outdated
- FR16: System falls back to REST API polling when WebSocket is unavailable
- FR17: User sees a dark-themed interface with crypto-aesthetic styling on page load
- FR18: User experiences no flash of unstyled or light-themed content on initial load
- FR19: User sees smooth entry animations when data first loads
- FR20: User sees no layout shift as dynamic content populates
- FR21: User can view all dashboard data on desktop screens with a horizontal layout
- FR22: User can view all dashboard data on mobile screens with a vertically stacked layout
- FR23: Dashboard is usable and readable across all supported modern browsers

### NonFunctional Requirements

- NFR1: Page load (DOMContentLoaded) completes in < 2 seconds on a 4G connection
- NFR2: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) occurs in < 1.5 seconds
- NFR3: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) = 0 — no visible layout shift during data hydration
- NFR4: Price updates render to DOM within 1 second of WebSocket message receipt
- NFR5: All animations run at 60 FPS with no dropped frames
- NFR6: Lighthouse performance score ≥ 90
- NFR7: Total hydrated JavaScript bundle < 100KB gzipped
- NFR8: Chart library (lightweight-charts) < 50KB gzipped
- NFR9: Binance WebSocket connection establishes within 500ms of island hydration under normal network conditions
- NFR10: WebSocket reconnection uses exponential backoff starting at 1 second, capping at 30 seconds
- NFR11: CoinGecko REST API polling interval ≥ 60 seconds to stay within free tier rate limits (30 req/min)
- NFR12: System tolerates CoinGecko API unavailability by displaying last known data with a timestamp
- NFR13: Binance 24-hour connection expiry handled transparently as a standard reconnect cycle
- NFR14: Fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention
- NFR15: Dashboard remains functional (displays last known data) when all external APIs are unreachable
- NFR16: No JavaScript errors or white screens on WebSocket disconnection
- NFR17: Browser tab remains stable during extended sessions (no memory leaks over 24+ hours)

### Additional Requirements

- Starter template: `npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict` — specified in Architecture as Epic 1 Story 1
- Tailwind CSS v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin with CSS-first `@theme` configuration (no `tailwind.config.js`)
- Nano Stores (~1KB) as the reactive state layer shared across Astro islands (Astro's officially recommended approach)
- Vanilla TypeScript islands — no UI framework; islands use Astro `<script>` tags with `client:load` for direct DOM updates
- WebSocket ConnectionManager as a singleton managing the entire WebSocket lifecycle with fallback chain
- `theme-constants.ts` for lightweight-charts JS API color sync with Tailwind `@theme` tokens (duplicated values, acceptable for v1)
- Shared TypeScript interfaces in `src/lib/types.ts`: `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, `ConnectionStatus`
- Unidirectional data flow: External API → Service → Store → Component → DOM
- Memory leak prevention: listener cleanup on reconnect, chart data capped at 24 entries, timer reference management with `AbortController`
- ESLint (`eslint-plugin-astro`) + Prettier (`prettier-plugin-astro`) + Vitest from first commit
- Playwright for e2e smoke test ("page loads, price appears")
- Package manager: npm (explicit choice)
- Node 22+ required (Astro 6.x)
- Co-located test files with `.test.ts` suffix
- No barrel files (`utils/index.ts`) — import directly from specific modules
- Recursive `setTimeout` for REST polling (not `setInterval`) to prevent drift on slow responses

### UX Design Requirements

- UX-DR1: Implement design token system — semantic color tokens (`bg-base` #0a0a0f, `bg-card` #12121a, `bg-card-hover` #1a1a2e, `accent-primary` #00f5ff cyan, `accent-secondary` #8b5cf6 purple, `accent-glow` rgba(0,245,255,0.15), `price-up` #22c55e, `price-down` #ef4444, `price-flash-up` #00f5ff, `price-flash-down` #ef4444, `text-primary` #f0f0f0, `text-secondary` #a0a0b0, `text-muted` #606070, `status-live` #22c55e, `status-reconnecting` #f59e0b, `status-stale` #ef4444) via Tailwind `@theme` directives in `src/styles/global.css`
- UX-DR2: Implement typography system — JetBrains Mono (`font-price`) for all numerical data, Inter (`font-label`) for labels/status text. Type scale: price 48px/36px weight 600, values 20-24px weight 500, card labels 12px Inter weight 400 `text-secondary`, status 11px Inter weight 400 `text-muted`, chart tooltip 13px JetBrains Mono. Line heights: 1.1 prices, 1.4 labels, 1.3 status
- UX-DR3: Implement glassmorphism card pattern (`.card-glass`) — `bg-card` background + `backdrop-blur(12px)` + 1px border `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` + 16px border-radius + optional `accent-secondary` glow (`box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(139,92,246,0.1)`)
- UX-DR4: Implement Price Card component — label "Bitcoin BTC/USD", live price at 48px desktop/36px mobile, change row with arrow symbol (↑/↓) + percentage (+2.3%) + absolute (+$1,534.22) in `price-up`/`price-down` colors. States: Live (color flash 400ms), Stale (badge), Loading (label visible, data fades in 300ms)
- UX-DR5: Implement Market Cap Card component — label "Market Cap", value abbreviated ($1.34T) at 24px weight 500, secondary "24h Range" label with range value at 14px. Silent 60s refresh, no flash animation
- UX-DR6: Implement Chart Card component — lightweight-charts with line color `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), area gradient (rgba(0,245,255,0.1) top, transparent bottom), grid lines rgba(255,255,255,0.03), crosshair rgba(255,255,255,0.3), transparent background. Hover crosshair with price+time tooltip (13px JetBrains Mono). Draw animation 500ms. Full width spanning both grid columns on desktop
- UX-DR7: Implement Status Indicator component — 6px colored dot + state text at 11px `text-muted`, centered bottom of viewport. States: Live (green dot + "Live"), Reconnecting (amber pulsing dot 1.5s cycle + "Reconnecting..."), Stale (red static dot + "Connection lost"), Fallback (green dot + "Live — CoinCap"). State transitions fade 300ms
- UX-DR8: Implement Page Shell — full-viewport `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) background, CSS Grid `1.5fr 1fr` desktop / single column mobile, 640px breakpoint, max-width 720px container, vertical centering on desktop, top-aligned on mobile. Spacing: 32px page padding desktop, 16px mobile, 24px card gaps
- UX-DR9: Implement animation system — data fade-in 300ms ease-out, chart draw 500ms ease-out, price tick flash 400ms ease-out, state transitions 300ms ease-in-out, reconnecting pulse 1.5s ease-in-out, card hover glow 300ms ease. No animation exceeds 500ms. No bounce/spring/overshoot. All respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (0ms durations). Price flash throttled to max 1 per 200ms
- UX-DR10: Implement FOUC prevention — set `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) on `<html>` element directly, dark theme as first paint. Empty glassmorphism cards with labels as the loading state (no spinners, no skeletons). Reserved space for all dynamic content (CLS = 0)
- UX-DR11: Implement responsive design — desktop-first with single 640px breakpoint (`sm:` Tailwind prefix). Price 48px→36px, grid collapses to single column. No `vh` units on mobile (use `dvh` or `min-h-screen`). Chart auto-resizes via `ResizeObserver`. No features hidden on mobile. CSS-only responsive logic

## Epic List

### Epic 2: Live Bitcoin Price Display

The user gets the full market picture — market cap, 24h range, and a 24h sparkline chart with hover tooltips — completing the dashboard's information offering.

## Epic 1: Project Foundation & Dashboard Shell

The user sees a polished, responsive dark crypto dashboard that loads instantly — the visual identity and layout are complete, ready for live data.

### Story 1.1: Project Initialization & Tooling Setup

As a developer,
I want a fully scaffolded Astro project with TypeScript, Tailwind, and code quality tooling,
So that I can start building components with a consistent, well-configured dev environment from the first commit.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a clean project directory
**When** the initialization commands are run
**Then** Astro 6.x is installed with the minimal template and TypeScript strict mode
**And** Tailwind CSS v4 is configured via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin in `astro.config.mjs`
**And** `lightweight-charts`, `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono`, `@fontsource/inter`, and `nanostores` are installed
**And** ESLint with `eslint-plugin-astro`, Prettier with `prettier-plugin-astro`, and Vitest are configured
**And** the project structure follows Architecture spec: `src/pages/`, `src/layouts/`, `src/components/`, `src/lib/stores/`, `src/lib/services/`, `src/lib/utils/`, `src/styles/`
**And** `src/lib/types.ts` defines `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, and `ConnectionStatus` interfaces
**And** `npm run dev` starts the Astro dev server successfully
**And** `npm run build` produces a static output in `dist/`

### Story 1.2: Design Token System & Typography

As a developer,
I want a complete design token system with semantic colors, typography, and shared theme constants,
So that all components use a consistent dark crypto visual identity and the chart library stays in sync.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Astro project from Story 1.1
**When** `src/styles/global.css` is created with Tailwind `@theme` directives
**Then** all semantic color tokens are defined: `bg-base` (#0a0a0f), `bg-card` (#12121a), `bg-card-hover` (#1a1a2e), `accent-primary` (#00f5ff), `accent-secondary` (#8b5cf6), `accent-glow` (rgba(0,245,255,0.15)), `price-up` (#22c55e), `price-down` (#ef4444), `price-flash-up` (#00f5ff), `price-flash-down` (#ef4444), `text-primary` (#f0f0f0), `text-secondary` (#a0a0b0), `text-muted` (#606070), `status-live` (#22c55e), `status-reconnecting` (#f59e0b), `status-stale` (#ef4444)
**And** JetBrains Mono is configured as `font-price` and Inter as `font-label` via `@fontsource` imports
**And** the type scale is defined: price 48px weight 600, values 20-24px weight 500, labels 12px weight 400, status 11px weight 400
**And** `src/lib/theme-constants.ts` exports duplicated color values for lightweight-charts JS API configuration
**And** `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, and `.status-badge` `@apply` compositions are defined (max 3)
**And** CSS custom properties for runtime animation values (`--price-flash-up`, `--price-flash-down`) are defined in `:root`
**And** tokens are usable via Tailwind utility classes in any component

### Story 1.3: Page Shell with Responsive Layout

As a user,
I want the dashboard to load instantly with a dark background and a responsive card grid layout,
So that I never see a flash of white and the page looks polished on both desktop and mobile.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a user navigates to the dashboard URL
**When** the page loads
**Then** `<html>` has `lang="en"` and `bg-base` (#0a0a0f) set directly to prevent FOUC (FR18)
**And** `Layout.astro` renders a full-viewport dark background with a centered content container (max-width 720px)
**And** the layout uses CSS Grid with `grid-template-columns: 1.5fr 1fr` and 24px gaps on desktop (≥640px) (FR21)
**And** the layout collapses to a single-column stack with 16px horizontal padding on mobile (<640px) (FR22)
**And** the container is vertically centered on desktop and top-aligned on mobile
**And** no `vh` units are used on mobile — `dvh` or `min-h-screen` is used instead
**And** responsive behavior is CSS-only with no JavaScript (Tailwind `sm:` breakpoint)
**And** the page renders correctly on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge latest 2 versions (FR23)
**And** `index.astro` imports `Layout.astro` and serves as the single page entry

### Story 1.4: Empty Dashboard Cards

As a user,
I want to see the dashboard layout with labeled card placeholders immediately on page load,
So that the page feels complete and no layout shift occurs when data arrives later.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a user loads the dashboard
**When** the static shell renders (before any data)
**Then** four glassmorphism cards are visible: Price Card, Market Cap Card, Chart Card, Status Indicator area
**And** each card uses the `.card-glass` pattern: `bg-card` + `backdrop-blur(12px)` + 1px border rgba(255,255,255,0.08) + 16px border-radius
**And** Price Card displays label "Bitcoin BTC/USD" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
**And** Market Cap Card displays label "Market Cap" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
**And** Chart Card displays label "24h Chart" in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px and spans full grid width on desktop
**And** each card has reserved space (fixed height) for its data values so CLS = 0 when data populates (FR20)
**And** Price Card has `<section aria-label="Bitcoin price">` with `<output aria-live="polite">` placeholder
**And** Market Cap Card has `<section aria-label="Market data">`
**And** Chart Card has `<section aria-label="24 hour price chart">`
**And** Status Indicator area has `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` at the bottom of the viewport
**And** the dark-themed crypto aesthetic is visually complete — near-black background, glassmorphism cards, neon accents visible (FR17)

## Epic 2: Live Bitcoin Price Display

The user can glance at the dashboard (or tab title) and instantly know the current BTC price, its direction, and 24h change — updated in real-time.

### Story 2.1: Nano Stores & Number Formatting Utilities

As a developer,
I want reactive stores and consistent number formatting helpers in place,
So that all components share a single source of truth for data and display values identically.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the project structure from Epic 1
**When** the store and utility files are created
**Then** `src/lib/stores/price-store.ts` exports `priceStore` as `atom<PriceTick | null>(null)`
**And** `src/lib/stores/status-store.ts` exports `statusStore` as `atom<ConnectionStatus>('connecting')`
**And** `src/lib/stores/market-store.ts` exports `marketStore` as `atom<MarketData | null>(null)`
**And** `src/lib/stores/chart-store.ts` exports `chartStore` as `atom<ChartPoint[]>([])`
**And** every store is typed with explicit generics and initializes to `null` or empty (never undefined)
**And** `src/lib/utils/format-utils.ts` exports: `formatPrice()` → `$68,432.17`, `formatPercent()` → `+2.3%` or `-1.8%`, `formatAbsoluteChange()` → `+$1,534.22`, `formatMarketCap()` → `$1.34T`, `formatRange()` → `$66.9k — $68.9k`, `formatChartTooltip()` → `$68,432.17 at 14:30`
**And** all formatters use `Intl.NumberFormat` as the base — no manual string building
**And** `format-utils.test.ts` covers all formatters with positive, negative, zero, and edge cases
**And** tests pass via `npm run test`

### Story 2.2: WebSocket Connection Manager (Binance)

As a user,
I want the dashboard to connect to a live Bitcoin data source automatically on page load,
So that I see real-time price updates without any action.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the dashboard page loads and islands hydrate
**When** the ConnectionManager initializes
**Then** it connects to Binance WebSocket at `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` (FR11)
**And** raw Binance messages (`{ p: "68432.17", ... }`) are normalized to `PriceTick` format: `{ price: number, timestamp: number, direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral' }`
**And** direction is computed by comparing to the previous price
**And** normalized `PriceTick` is written to `priceStore`
**And** `statusStore` is updated to `'live'` on successful connection
**And** `statusStore` is set to `'connecting'` during initial connection attempt
**And** the ConnectionManager is a singleton — only one WebSocket connection exists at any time
**And** `connection-manager.ts` never exposes raw WebSocket data to consumers
**And** old WebSocket listeners are cleaned up before creating new connections (memory leak prevention)
**And** services initialize via a `<script>` block in `Layout.astro`
**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers message normalization and direction computation

### Story 2.3: Live Price Card with Direction Indicator

As a user,
I want to see the current BTC price updating live with visual direction feedback,
So that I can glance at the dashboard and instantly know the price and whether it's moving up or down.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Price Card island is hydrated and priceStore has data
**When** a new PriceTick arrives in priceStore
**Then** the price displays in `font-price` at 48px desktop / 36px mobile, weight 600, `text-primary` color (FR1)
**And** a direction arrow is shown: `↑` in `price-up` green for up, `↓` in `price-down` red for down (FR3, FR5)
**And** the price text flashes cyan (`price-flash-up`) on price increase or red (`price-flash-down`) on decrease, fading to white over 400ms ease-out (UX-DR9)
**And** flash frequency is throttled to max 1 per 200ms to prevent rapid flashing
**And** 24h change displays as percentage (`+2.3%`) and absolute value (`+$1,534.22`) using format-utils (FR4)
**And** 24h change values use `price-up` green for positive and `price-down` red for negative (FR5)
**And** all number formatting goes through `format-utils.ts` — no inline formatting
**And** on initial data load, the price fades in with a 300ms ease-out animation (FR19)
**And** all animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant transitions when enabled)
**And** the `<output aria-live="polite">` element updates so screen readers announce price changes

### Story 2.4: Tab Title Price Updates

As a user,
I want the browser tab title to show the current BTC price,
So that I can see the price without switching to the Metrics tab.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the dashboard is live and priceStore is receiving updates
**When** a new PriceTick arrives
**Then** the browser tab title updates to show the formatted price, e.g. `$68,432.17 — Metrics` (FR2)
**And** the tab title uses the same `formatPrice()` function from format-utils for consistency
**And** the tab title updates on every price tick (not throttled separately from the display)
**And** if priceStore is `null` (no data yet), the tab title shows `Metrics` without a price

## Epic 3: Market Context & Price Chart

The user gets the full market picture — market cap, 24h range, and a 24h sparkline chart with hover tooltips — completing the dashboard's information offering.

### Story 3.1: Market Data Service (CoinGecko REST Polling)

As a user,
I want the dashboard to fetch market data automatically in the background,
So that I see up-to-date market cap, 24h change, and chart data without any action.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the dashboard page loads and services initialize
**When** the MarketDataService starts
**Then** it fetches market cap, 24h change (percentage + absolute), 24h high/low, and hourly historical price data from CoinGecko REST API
**And** `marketStore` is populated with a `MarketData` object: `{ marketCap, change24h, changePercent24h, high24h, low24h }`
**And** `chartStore` is populated with an array of `ChartPoint` objects: `{ time: number, value: number }` for hourly data
**And** `chartStore` is capped at 24 entries maximum — oldest point shifted when appending new data
**And** polling uses recursive `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) with a 60-second interval (NFR11)
**And** on CoinGecko API failure, last known store values are retained — stores are never cleared (NFR12)
**And** API errors are logged via `console.warn()` with endpoint and status code — no user-facing error UI
**And** timer references are stored and cleared before scheduling new timers (memory leak prevention)
**And** `market-data-service.test.ts` covers data mapping, store updates, and error retention behavior

### Story 3.2: Market Cap Card

As a user,
I want to see the current Bitcoin market cap and 24h price range at a glance,
So that I have broader market context alongside the live price.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Market Cap Card island is hydrated and marketStore has data
**When** marketStore updates with new MarketData
**Then** market cap displays as an abbreviated value (e.g. `$1.34T`) in `font-price`, `text-primary`, 24px weight 500 (FR6)
**And** "Market Cap" label is shown in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
**And** 24h range displays as `$66.9k — $68.9k` in `font-price`, `text-primary`, 14px
**And** "24h Range" secondary label is shown in `font-label`, `text-secondary`, 12px
**And** all values use `formatMarketCap()` and `formatRange()` from format-utils — no inline formatting
**And** values update silently every 60s with no flash animation (FR7)
**And** on initial data load, values fade in with 300ms ease-out animation
**And** when marketStore is `null` (no data yet), labels are visible but value areas are empty (reserved space)

### Story 3.3: 24h Sparkline Chart with Hover Tooltip

As a user,
I want to see a 24-hour price trend chart that I can hover for details,
So that I can quickly read the trend shape and check specific price points.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Chart Card island is hydrated and chartStore has hourly data
**When** chartStore populates with ChartPoint array
**Then** a lightweight-charts area series renders with line color `accent-primary` (#00f5ff) (FR8)
**And** area fill uses gradient: rgba(0,245,255,0.1) top to transparent bottom
**And** chart grid lines are rgba(255,255,255,0.03), crosshair rgba(255,255,255,0.3)
**And** chart background is transparent (card provides the background)
**And** all chart colors are sourced from `theme-constants.ts` — no hardcoded values in component
**And** chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period (FR10)
**And** on hover, a crosshair appears with a tooltip showing price and time formatted as `$68,432.17 at 14:30` using `formatChartTooltip()` (FR9)
**And** on initial data load, the chart draws left-to-right with a 500ms ease-out animation (UX-DR6)
**And** the chart auto-resizes via lightweight-charts built-in resize handling on viewport change
**And** the chart card spans full grid width (`grid-column: 1 / -1`) on desktop
**And** chart canvas has `aria-hidden="true"` — price data is accessible via Price Card
**And** animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant draw when enabled)
**And** when chartStore updates with new data, the chart appends points without full redraw or flicker

## Epic 4: Connection Resilience & Reliability

The dashboard never breaks — WebSocket failures are handled silently with fallback sources, automatic reconnection, and clear stale data indication. The user can trust the data is always there.

### Story 4.1: WebSocket Reconnection with Exponential Backoff

As a user,
I want the dashboard to automatically recover from connection drops without my intervention,
So that the live price feed resumes seamlessly after network interruptions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the ConnectionManager has an active Binance WebSocket connection
**When** the WebSocket connection drops (error, close, or network failure)
**Then** `statusStore` transitions to `'reconnecting'` (FR14)
**And** reconnection attempts begin with exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s cap (NFR10)
**And** on successful reconnection, `statusStore` transitions back to `'live'`
**And** the previous WebSocket instance is fully closed (`ws.close()`) and its reference nulled before creating a new connection (memory leak prevention, NFR17)
**And** no JavaScript errors or white screens occur during disconnection or reconnection (NFR16)
**And** Binance 24-hour connection expiry is handled as a standard disconnect/reconnect cycle — no special logic (NFR13)
**And** the last known price remains displayed in the Price Card during reconnection (NFR15)
**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers backoff timing, listener cleanup, and reconnection state transitions

### Story 4.2: CoinCap Fallback & REST Polling Fallback

As a user,
I want the dashboard to try alternative data sources if the primary one fails,
So that I always see price data regardless of which service is available.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the ConnectionManager fails to connect or reconnect to Binance
**When** Binance WebSocket is unavailable after reconnection attempts
**Then** the ConnectionManager attempts to connect to CoinCap WebSocket at `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin` (FR12)
**And** CoinCap messages (`{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }`) are normalized to the same `PriceTick` format
**And** on successful CoinCap connection, `statusStore` transitions to `'fallback'`
**And** fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention (NFR14)
**And** if both Binance and CoinCap WebSockets fail, the system falls back to REST polling via the MarketDataService for price data (FR16)
**And** during REST-only mode, `statusStore` reflects `'stale'` if no update received within 30 seconds
**And** the dashboard remains functional displaying last known data when all external APIs are unreachable (NFR15)
**And** when a higher-priority source becomes available again, the ConnectionManager reconnects to it
**And** `connection-manager.test.ts` covers the full fallback chain: Binance → CoinCap → REST

### Story 4.3: Status Indicator & Stale Data Badge

As a user,
I want to see a subtle connection status indicator and know when displayed data may be outdated,
So that I can trust the data when it's live and be aware when it's stale.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Status Indicator island is hydrated and subscribes to statusStore
**When** statusStore changes value
**Then** the indicator displays at the bottom center of the viewport as a 6px dot + text at 11px `text-muted` (UX-DR7)
**And** Live state shows: green dot (`status-live`) + "Live" text — barely visible, quiet good state
**And** Reconnecting state shows: amber dot (`status-reconnecting`) + "Reconnecting..." text with dot pulsing animation (1.5s ease-in-out cycle, opacity 0.4–1.0) (FR14)
**And** Stale state shows: red dot (`status-stale`) + "Connection lost" text, static (no animation)
**And** Fallback state shows: green dot (`status-live`) + "Live — CoinCap" text
**And** state transitions fade over 300ms ease-in-out — no abrupt switches
**And** when statusStore is `'stale'` (no price update for >30 seconds), a "Stale" badge appears on the Price Card in `status-stale` color (FR15)
**And** the stale badge does not hide or replace the price value — it's an addition
**And** when connection restores and a new price tick arrives, the stale badge fades out over 300ms
**And** all animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant transitions, no pulsing)
**And** the Status Indicator uses `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` so screen readers announce state changes

<teammate-message teammate_id="devils-advocate" color="cyan" summary="Cross-cutting review: 2 blocking, 1 medium, 2 minor issues found">
Devil's Advocate cross-cutting review complete. Here are my findings, organized by severity.

---

## BLOCKING ISSUES

### 1. `connection-manager.ts` does not exist — test file references a missing module

The test file at `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/services/connection-manager.test.ts` (line 25-27) imports from `./connection-manager`, but **no `connection-manager.ts` file exists** in the services directory. The test was written ahead of the implementation. Tasks #1 (implement Story 2.2) and #13 (implement Story 4.1) are marked in_progress, so this may be expected — but it means the tests cannot currently pass.

### 2. `market-data-service.ts` has no corresponding test file

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/services/market-data-service.ts` exists but `market-data-service.test.ts` does not. Story 3.1 acceptance criteria explicitly require: "market-data-service.test.ts covers data mapping, store updates, and error retention behavior." Task #7 is in_progress so this may still be coming.

---

## SIGNIFICANT ISSUES

### 3. No barrel files — PASS (no violations found)

No `index.ts` files found anywhere in the source tree. All imports go directly to specific modules. This rule is followed correctly.

### 4. Unidirectional data flow — PASS (with caveat)

Stores are simple atoms with no writes from components. The `.astro` components are all static HTML shells with no `<script>` blocks yet — hydration hasn't been implemented. The market-data-service correctly writes to stores from the service layer. **However**, since no island scripts exist yet, we cannot verify that future component implementations won't violate this rule. The architecture is set up correctly for unidirectional flow.

### 5. CLS = 0 — Potential risk in Astro components

The card components do reserve space with `min-h-[...]` classes:
- `PriceCard.astro` line 9: `min-h-[53px]` for price output, line 13: `min-h-[24px]` for change row
- `MarketCapCard.astro` line 9: `min-h-[28px]` for market cap value, line 15: `min-h-[20px]` for range
- `ChartCard.astro` line 9: `min-h-[200px]` for chart canvas

**Issue**: The `min-h` values are magic numbers. If the actual rendered content exceeds these minimums (e.g., price text at 48px/36px with line-height 1.1 = ~53px on desktop but could shift on certain font loading scenarios), there could be CLS. The 53px for PriceCard is tight — JetBrains Mono at 48px with line-height 1.1 = 52.8px, leaving only 0.2px margin. If font metrics differ slightly across browsers, this could cause a sub-pixel shift. **Not blocking but fragile.**

### 6. Performance budget — 100KB gzipped is realistic

Dependencies from `package.json`:
- `nanostores`: ~1KB gzipped
- `lightweight-charts`: ~40KB gzipped
- `@fontsource` fonts: ~20-25KB for subset Latin (400+500+600 JetBrains Mono + 400 Inter)
- Application code: minimal

Estimated total: ~65-70KB gzipped. Well within 100KB budget. **PASS.**

### 7. WebSocket reconnection backoff — cannot verify (connection-manager.ts missing)

Since `connection-manager.ts` doesn't exist yet, I cannot verify:
- Exponential backoff implementation (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s cap)
- Whether reconnect success mid-backoff clears the timer
- Whether the stale threshold (30s) is implemented
- Listener cleanup on reconnection
- Fallback chain (Binance -> CoinCap -> REST)

The test file (`connection-manager.test.ts`) tests basic connect/disconnect/cleanup behavior but does **not** test backoff timing or reconnection scenarios. Lines 85-302 cover: singleton pattern, basic connect, message normalization, cleanup on reconnect/disconnect. **Missing test coverage for**: backoff timing, stale threshold, fallback chain, mid-backoff reconnection.

### 8. Memory leak prevention in MarketDataService — mostly covered

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/services/market-data-service.ts`:
- Lines 52-55: `stop()` clears timer — GOOD
- Lines 63-68: `scheduleNext()` clears existing timer before scheduling new one — GOOD
- Uses recursive `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) per architecture spec — GOOD
- **Missing**: No `AbortController` for fetch cancellation. If `stop()` is called mid-fetch, the in-flight `fetch()` calls (lines 73, 100) will complete and potentially write to stores after the service is "stopped". This is a minor leak vector for long-running sessions.

### 9. `chartStore` cap at 24 entries — partially implemented

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/services/market-data-service.ts` line 111: `chartStore.set(chartPoints)` replaces the entire array each time (full replacement from API response). The `sampleHourlyPoints` function (lines 119-128) caps at `CHART_MAX_ENTRIES = 24`. **However**, the architecture spec says "When appending a new point, shift the oldest" — suggesting incremental updates. The current implementation does a full replace on each 60s poll, which is fine for now but doesn't handle the append case if the service is later modified to add individual points. **Acceptable for v1.**

---

## CONTRADICTIONS BETWEEN IMPLEMENTATIONS

### 10. `statusStore` initial value mismatch

- `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/stores/status-store.ts` line 4: initializes to `'connecting'`
- `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/types.ts` line 20: `ConnectionStatus` type includes `'connecting'`
- `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/components/StatusIndicator.astro` lines 6-9: renders "Live" text and green dot as static HTML

**Contradiction**: The store starts as `'connecting'` but the static HTML shows "Live" with a green dot. When the island hydrates and subscribes to statusStore, it should show "Connecting..." initially, not "Live". The static HTML creates a flash of incorrect state — the user sees "Live" then it would change to "Connecting..." when JS hydrates, then back to "Live" when WebSocket connects. This is a **CLS/UX issue** — the initial static render disagrees with the initial store state.

### 11. Store shape consistency — PASS

All stores use the same interfaces defined in `types.ts`. The `MarketDataService` correctly maps CoinGecko response fields to the `MarketData` interface. No mismatches found between store definitions and service writes.

---

## SUMMARY

| # | Issue | Severity | Status |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| 1 | `connection-manager.ts` missing | Blocking | Implementation in progress (task #1) |
| 2 | `market-data-service.test.ts` missing | Blocking | Implementation in progress (task #7) |
| 3 | No barrel files rule | Pass | Clean |
| 4 | Unidirectional data flow | Pass (pending) | No violations, but islands not yet implemented |
| 5 | CLS = 0 price card min-h | Minor risk | 0.2px margin on 48px font |
| 6 | 100KB bundle budget | Pass | ~65-70KB estimated |
| 7 | Backoff edge cases | Unverifiable | Connection manager not yet implemented |
| 8 | MarketDataService fetch cancellation | Minor | No AbortController for in-flight fetches |
| 9 | Chart store 24-entry cap | Pass | Full replace strategy works for v1 |
| 10 | StatusIndicator static HTML vs store initial state | Medium | "Live" in HTML but store starts as "connecting" |
| 11 | Store shape consistency | Pass | No mismatches |

**Most actionable finding**: Issue #10 (StatusIndicator.astro showing "Live" while statusStore initializes to "connecting") is a real contradiction that will cause a visible state flash on page load. The static HTML should either show "Connecting" or the store should initialize to a state matching the static HTML.
</teammate-message>

<teammate-message teammate_id="devils-advocate" color="cyan" summary="Second pass complete: 2 medium, 4 minor issues; architecture fully compliant">
Devil's Advocate second pass complete. All 104 tests pass. Here are my findings after examining the full codebase with all implementations in place.

---

## PREVIOUSLY FLAGGED ISSUES — STATUS UPDATE

**Issue #1 (connection-manager.ts missing)**: RESOLVED. Now exists at 352 lines with comprehensive implementation.

**Issue #2 (market-data-service.test.ts missing)**: RESOLVED. 16 tests covering singleton, fetch, error retention, abort, and sampling.

**Issue #10 (StatusIndicator static HTML mismatch)**: RESOLVED. `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/components/StatusIndicator.astro` line 11-14 now shows "Connecting..." with amber pulsing dot, matching the store's initial `'connecting'` state.

**Issue #8 (No AbortController)**: RESOLVED. `/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/services/market-data-service.ts` lines 29, 49, 54-56, 82-83, 111-112 now use AbortController with signal passed to fetch. Test at line 229-257 verifies abort behavior.

---

## NEW ISSUES FOUND IN SECOND PASS

### 1. MEDIUM: Promotion check creates orphaned WebSocket on `connect()` call

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/services/connection-manager.ts` lines 285-311:

`attemptPromotion()` creates a test WebSocket to probe Binance availability. On success (line 301), it calls `this.connect()`. But `connect()` at line 102-108 calls `this.connectBinance()` which calls `this.cleanup()` at line 111 — this cleans up the *main* WebSocket but NOT the test WebSocket from `attemptPromotion()`. The test WS was already closed on line 300 (`testWs.close()`), so it's fine... BUT there's a race condition:

If the test WebSocket's `onopen` fires AND the main CoinCap connection drops at roughly the same time, `scheduleReconnect()` could fire from the CoinCap `onclose` while `connect()` from promotion is also running. This is unlikely but could result in two simultaneous connection attempts. The cleanup() call in connectBinance() should handle it, but the timing is worth noting.

### 2. MEDIUM: `previousPrice` reset on `connect()` may cause false "neutral" after promotion

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/services/connection-manager.ts` line 105: `connect()` resets `this.previousPrice = null`. This means after a successful promotion from CoinCap back to Binance, the first price tick will always show `direction: 'neutral'` even if the price actually moved. The user was seeing live CoinCap prices, then after promotion the first Binance tick appears as neutral — a momentary loss of direction context. This is a known trade-off (team-lead mentioned dev-carol fixed this), but I want to be explicit: the alternative (keeping previousPrice across reconnects) risks comparing stale prices across different time windows, which could be worse.

### 3. MINOR: `toChartData` function in ChartCard.astro has no TypeScript types

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/components/ChartCard.astro` line 89: `function toChartData(points)` — the `points` parameter has no type annotation. In a TypeScript strict project this may compile fine inside Astro `<script>` blocks (Astro handles island scripts differently), but it's inconsistent with the project's strict TypeScript posture.

### 4. MINOR: `document.querySelector` race condition in StatusIndicator

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/components/StatusIndicator.astro` lines 69-70: `document.querySelector('.status-dot')` and `document.querySelector('.status-text')` query by class name globally on the document. If multiple StatusIndicator instances were ever rendered (unlikely but architecturally unguarded), these selectors would grab the first match, not necessarily the correct one. Other components (PriceCard, MarketCapCard) have the same pattern. This is fine for a single-page app with one instance of each component, but is brittle.

### 5. MINOR: Tab title updater uses `subscribe()` — no unsubscribe cleanup

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/lib/services/tab-title-updater.ts` line 8: `priceStore.subscribe(...)` returns an unsubscribe function that is never stored. For a page that never unmounts this is fine, but it's inconsistent with the architecture's emphasis on cleanup. Same applies to all component `<script>` block subscriptions — none store unsubscribe handles. Since Astro islands don't have a teardown lifecycle, this is acceptable, but worth noting for NFR17 (24h+ sessions): nanostores subscriptions do hold references that prevent GC if the subscriber callback closes over DOM elements that are later removed.

### 6. MINOR: `stale-badge` in PriceCard is always in the DOM

`/Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/src/components/PriceCard.astro` lines 32-37: The stale badge is always rendered with `opacity: 0; visibility: hidden`. It takes up no visual space due to visibility:hidden, but it IS in the DOM and accessible to screen readers (it has `role="status"` but the text "Stale" might be read by some assistive tech even when visibility:hidden). Consider `aria-hidden="true"` when not visible, toggled alongside the `visible` class.

---

## ARCHITECTURE COMPLIANCE CHECKLIST

| Rule | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| No barrel files | PASS | No `index.ts` found anywhere in src/ |
| Unidirectional data flow | PASS | All store writes are in services; components only read via `.subscribe()` |
| Services-only store writes | PASS | Verified: connection-manager.ts writes priceStore/statusStore; market-data-service.ts writes marketStore/chartStore; no store.set() calls in any .astro component |
| CLS = 0 | PASS | PriceCard uses fixed `h-[42px] sm:h-[56px]` (not min-h); MarketCapCard uses min-h reservations; ChartCard uses min-h-[200px] |
| Performance budget (<100KB) | PASS | Dependencies total ~65KB gzipped |
| Backoff: exponential 1s-30s cap | PASS | Lines 9-11, 215-219: `INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS * (BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER ** attempt)`, capped at MAX_BACKOFF_MS=30000 |
| Backoff: timer cleared on reconnect success | PASS | Lines 119-122 (Binance onopen) and 169-172 (CoinCap onopen) clear reconnectTimerId |
| Fallback chain: Binance -> CoinCap -> REST | PASS | Lines 228-237: MAX_BINANCE_RETRIES=3 then CoinCap, MAX_COINCAP_RETRIES=3 then REST |
| Stale threshold: 30s | PASS | Line 16: STALE_THRESHOLD_MS=30000, checked every 5s |
| Memory: WS cleanup on reconnect | PASS | cleanup() at lines 328-344 nulls all handlers, closes WS, clears all timers |
| Memory: chart capped at 24 entries | PASS | sampleHourlyPoints caps at CHART_MAX_ENTRIES=24 |
| Memory: timer cleanup | PASS | All 3 timer IDs (reconnect, stale, promotion) cleared in cleanup() |
| Memory: fetch abort | PASS | AbortController aborts in-flight fetches on stop() |
| Recursive setTimeout (not setInterval) | PASS | market-data-service.ts uses setTimeout in scheduleNext() |
| format-utils for all formatting | PASS | No inline Intl.NumberFormat in any component |
| prefers-reduced-motion | PASS | All 4 component stylesheets have @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) blocks |
| All stores typed with explicit generics | PASS | All 4 stores use atom<Type>() |
| Test co-location | PASS | All .test.ts files are next to their source files |
| Service init in Layout.astro | PASS | Lines 18-26: Layout.astro `<script>` initializes ConnectionManager, MarketDataService, and tab title updater |

---

## SUMMARY

| # | Issue | Severity | Action Needed |
|---|---|----------|---------------|
| 1 | Promotion check race condition | Medium | Unlikely in practice but could cause duplicate connections |
| 2 | previousPrice reset on promotion | Medium | Known trade-off, acceptable |
| 3 | Missing TypeScript types in ChartCard toChartData | Minor | Add type annotation |
| 4 | Global querySelector in all islands | Minor | Fine for single-page, would break with multiple instances |
| 5 | No unsubscribe cleanup in tab-title-updater or islands | Minor | Acceptable for never-unmounting page |
| 6 | Stale badge accessibility when hidden | Minor | Add aria-hidden toggle |

**Overall assessment**: The implementation is solid. All architecture rules are followed. The 3 devs' implementations are consistent with each other — no store shape mismatches, no contradictory patterns. Memory leak prevention is thorough (WS cleanup, timer cleanup, fetch abort, chart cap). The fixes from the first review (StatusIndicator static HTML, AbortController, PriceCard fixed height, previousPrice reset) are all properly implemented. The remaining issues are minor edge cases, not blocking concerns.
</teammate-message>

Good question. In bmad, the right practice depends on the size of the change:

**For a targeted defect/fix like this**, the flow is:

1. **`/bmad-create-story`** — create a dedicated story file with full context (what's broken, acceptance criteria, files to change). This gives the dev agent everything needed without relying on conversation context.
2. **`/bmad-dev-story`** — implement against the story file
3. **`/bmad-code-review`** — review the targeted change

You don't need a full epic or a team of 7 for this. A single dev agent + single reviewer is the right scope.

**For the 2 minor fixes (#3 and #6)**, they're small enough to bundle into the same story as the race condition fix — one story, one implementation, one review.
